text:
IMPARTIAL NEWS + INTELLIGENT DEBATE

Account

Many athletes have to work full time jobs to be able to compete though realistic GB medal hopefuls may receive National Lottery funding

TheParis Olympicshave already led to some unforgettable moments, fromTom Pidcock’s dramatic mountain bikingcomeback to win gold for Team GB, toSimone Biles making historywith her second all-around gymnastics crown.

As theathletics kicks into full force, there are plenty more medals to be handed out before the Games come to a close on Sunday 11 August.

But asTeam GBhas more moments on the podium, fans have been wondering what is in the gold box given to Olympic winners along with their medals. Here is everything you need to know, explained.

Spectators have noticed the slim gold box given to athletes – inside is the official poster for the 2024 Paris Olympics.

This is a bonus gift from the Paris organisers and a novel feature of winning the gold medal this year. The poster is a detailed drawing of Paris in its Olympic glory.

Unfortunately, the whole medal is not made of gold, meaning these prizes are much more valuable for their sentiment.

According to Oxford Economics, which factored in the current prices of the gold, silver and iron that make up the medal, the top Paris Olympic prize is worth £807.

However, the melt value for the six grams of real gold inside the medal is priced at approximately £360 with the silver which forms the rest of the alloy priced at roughly £368.51.

If the whole of the medal was made of gold, it would be worth more than £35,000 as global prices are at an all-time high.

Before the big names clinch their big-bucks sponsorships, many athletes have to find their way through funding and working full time.

Despite the glory of winning a medal, there is no financial prize given by the International Olympic Committee. However, for track and field events, World Athletics will give £38,967 to anyone who wins a gold medal.

Although it’s not the case for Team GB, some countries offer nearly £600,000 in bonuses for an Olympic gold. This includes France, which will give gold medallists £67,819 and Hungary which will award £116,566.

British athletes with a definite shot at a medal can receive up to £28,000 in funding each year, mainly from the National Lottery.

Hong Kong which competes independently of China, awards a prize of £597,300 for a gold medal and £295,500 for a silver medal.

Israel offers the second most generous prize for medal winners, at £213,900 for gold, £149,000 for silver and and £106,000 for bronze.

Serbia awards £169,900 to its gold medallists Malaysia £166,800 and Italy £152,800.

Impartial news + intelligent debate

title: What's in the gold box given to Olympic medal winners?
author: Emilia Randall
description: Many athletes have to work full time jobs to be able to compete though realistic GB medal hopefuls may receive National Lottery funding
